outcome.headline: Agent shipped a TF-IDF+Ridge essay-scoring submission tuned by eyeballed distribution matching, never once measuring the actual QWK metric, and the result fell below the 0.748 scoring floor (reward 0.0).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Reads /app/output/submission.csv, matches essay_id to hidden gold test labels, computes quadratic_weighted_kappa, then scales it between lower_bound 0.748 and upper_bound 0.829 (score=0 when QWK<=0.748).
outcome.what_agent_produced: TF-IDF (3000 features, 1-2 grams) + Ridge regression predictions, post-processed with percentile-based rounding to mimic the training score distribution; valid format (2597 rows, essay_id/score, range 1-6) but QWK below floor.

failure_modes.name: Shipped without ever validating the scoring metric (QWK)
failure_modes.description: A4 Verifying & testing — the agent ran only format checks (row count, columns, score range) before declaring done, and never measured quadratic weighted kappa on a holdout. Its single QWK-computing script was killed for being slow and never re-attempted, so it had zero evidence its submission cleared the 0.748 floor.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
All scores are in valid range (1-6)
- Distribution covers all score categories

The task is complete.

counterfactual.X: Run a quick K-fold QWK measurement on the working Ridge model before submitting
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have seen its QWK was below the 0.748 floor and known a materially stronger model/threshold optimization was required rather than declaring success blind

failure_modes.name: Distribution matching substituted for score optimization
failure_modes.description: The final artifact forced predictions into the training score distribution because the distribution looked better, which worked around missing score classes instead of improving or validating the predictor against quadratic weighted kappa.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: # Adjust distribution to better match training data

counterfactual.X: Replace the distribution-matching postprocess with a validated model and thresholding strategy selected by out-of-fold QWK.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would need more than a one-line or one-step correction to reach the hidden hard threshold reliably.

failure_modes.name: Distribution-matching substituted for metric optimization
failure_modes.description: After slow validation attempts, the agent finalized a heuristic that forced predictions to match the training label distribution rather than validating or optimizing quadratic weighted kappa, producing a valid file that failed the score gate.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
# Adjust distribution to better match training data
# Training distribution: 1:7.2%, 2:27.3%, 3:36.2%, 4:22.8%, 5:5.6%, 6:0.9%
# Use percentile-based rounding

counterfactual.X: Replace the distribution heuristic with a validated QWK-driven modeling and thresholding workflow using an efficient holdout or smaller CV search.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have selected based on measured leaderboard-relevant quality instead of apparent class coverage.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent model churn guided by eyeballed distribution instead of the metric
failure_modes.description: A5 Iterating & converging — the agent cycled through Ridge, RandomForest, LogisticRegression, GradientBoosting and back to Ridge across 8 scripts, judging each only by how the predicted score histogram looked, never by QWK. Lacking a metric signal, the iterations never approached the scoring target and settled on a percentile-matching heuristic that does not optimize agreement.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: This is the best distribution so far - it covers all 6 scores and the distribution is closer to the training data.

failure_modes.name: Distribution-matching chosen as objective instead of QWK
failure_modes.description: A3 Executing & generating — the agent selected the final solve8 because its percentile-thresholded predictions visually matched the training score distribution, a fallacious premise: matching the marginal score distribution does not maximize quadratic weighted kappa and discarded the simpler round-and-clip outputs without any measured comparison.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
# Adjust distribution to better match training data
# Training distribution: 1:7.2%, 2:27.3%, 3:36.2%, 4:22.8%, 5:5.6%, 6:0.9%
# Use percentile-based rounding

failure_modes.name: Optimized a proxy distribution instead of QWK accuracy
failure_modes.description: The final selected solution forced the marginal prediction distribution to resemble the training distribution and treated that as evidence of quality, but the scoring metric rewards per-essay agreement with hidden labels, not matching aggregate class counts.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: This is the best distribution so far - it covers all 6 scores and the distribution is closer to the training data.

counterfactual.X: Replace distribution matching with a validation-driven model selection process using held-out QWK and calibrated thresholds.
counterfactual.Y: This would have selected for the actual objective instead of a marginal class-count proxy, but still required a stronger model to pass.

failure_modes.name: No QWK validation before submit
failure_modes.description: The README and agent planning referenced quadratic weighted kappa, and solve3.py was started to optimize QWK thresholds with cross-validation, but that run was cancelled and no step ever printed a CV or holdout kappa. Before mark_task_complete the agent only checked CSV schema, row count, and score range 1-6.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The task is complete. The submission.csv has been saved to /app/output following the required format.

counterfactual.X: Run cross-validated quadratic weighted kappa on training predictions and require kappa >= 0.748 before mark_task_complete.
counterfactual.Y: Validated only file format, 2597 rows, and that scores were integers in 1-6, then declared success.

failure_modes.name: Skipped QWK validation
failure_modes.description: The agent knew the metric was quadratic weighted kappa but only checked file shape, row count, column names, and integer score range before calling mark_task_complete. It never ran cross-validated QWK or the task verifier, so a low-kappa submission shipped.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The task is complete. The submission.csv has been saved to /app/output/ following the required format.

counterfactual.X: Run a held-out or 5-fold CV quadratic weighted kappa check on train predictions before submitting.
counterfactual.Y: Verified only row count, columns, and that scores were integers in 1–6.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent model churn driven by distribution, not metric
failure_modes.description: Lacking a QWK signal, the agent cycled through Ridge, exhaustive threshold optimization, RandomForest/LogisticRegression classifiers, and GradientBoosting on numeric-only features, abandoning two attempts to timeout, and reverting to Ridge+percentile thresholding. Each strategy switch was triggered by the predicted-score distribution's appearance rather than measured performance, so it never approached the 0.748 floor.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Logistic Regression is taking too long with 8000+ features. I need to cancel and use a faster approach.

failure_modes.name: Non-converging model thrashing under the 1-CPU compute limit
failure_modes.description: A5 Iterating & converging — the agent cycled Ridge -> QWK threshold optimization -> RandomForest/LogReg -> GBM-on-numeric -> Ridge+percentile, repeatedly cancelling runs that were 'taking too long' on 1 CPU, and abandoned the QWK-threshold optimization (the technique that directly targets the metric) for a weaker distribution-matching heuristic
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The script is taking too long - likely stuck in the threshold optimization with 5 nested loops. I need to cancel it and create a more efficient solution.

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent built genuine ML pipelines on the real train/test data; percentile-based distribution matching is a legitimate modeling heuristic, not an exploit of the verifier. No gold-label access or shortcut was used.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier computes QWK against hidden gold test labels and scales between fixed bounds; submission must match gold essay_ids and label range, with score 0 below the 0.748 floor. No structural shortcut available.

notes_for_aggregation: Hard data-science ML-competition task with a threshold-scaled QWK gate (floor 0.748). Model produced a valid submission but a weak TF-IDF+Ridge baseline with percentile rounding does not clear the floor. Root cause: agent never measured QWK locally (the one attempt was killed for slowness under the 1-CPU limit) and steered iterations by visually inspecting the predicted distribution, converging on a heuristic with no metric feedback. Partial: real pipeline, valid output, but below scoring floor.

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
